behavior_summary: Provides logic for detecting and using an "Air" focus item. The small detector (AIRSPEL::0080) scans a collection of items and returns true if any item matches the Air-focus criteria (checks item frame==3 and item type bitmask).

The main handler (AIRSPEL::00CF) implements the "read/use focus" behaviour: it verifies and deducts NPC mana, optionally selects a 'breath' effect variant (based on global hasBreath or presence test), spawns oriented visual/sound/timed effects, awards a small intelligence reward, constructs a descriptive message about the focus reading (location fragment + time/day), and makes the item/NPC 'bark' that message to the player.
notable_control_flow: AIRSPEL::0080 loops over a collection of item references and returns immediately (true) when a matching Air-focus is found; otherwise returns false after iteration.
AIRSPEL::00CF branches early if the caster lacks sufficient mana; otherwise it subtracts cost and continues.

AIRSPEL::00CF branches based on the global hasBreath flag to choose between a 'breath' spawn sequence and the standard spawn sequence; it also consults U8P_AIRSPEL::AIRSPEL::0080 to decide on certain effect variants in some paths.

String construction in AIRSPEL::00CF is incremental: it maps the caster's map id to a location code (FREE::36E7), selects/appends a location fragment, then appends time/day text computed from TimeInGameHours and numToStr before calling Item/Npc::bark.
open_questions: What exact collection does AIRSPEL::0080 iterate? (inventory, equipped slots, nearby ground items, or a list returned by an engine call) — inferred but not explicit.
What does FREE::36E7 precisely return or compute from Npc::getMap (a normalized location id, a lookup index, or formatted text)?
What specific visual/audio effects are produced by METHOD::15E7 and FREE::29D8 spawn calls (spell projectile, breath cone, sound identifiers)?
Are there other global flags that influence which location fragment or day/time string is used beyond hasBreath?

main_function.name: U8P_AIRSPEL::0080
main_function.purpose: Scan a collection of items (likely an inventory or nearby item list) and detect whether any item matches the Air-focus criteria (item frame and type bitmask). Return 1 if a qualifying item is found, otherwise 0.
entry_conditions: Called when code needs to know whether an Air spell focus item is present (invoked from U8P_AIRSPEL::AIRSPEL::00CF and possibly other callers).
Executed as a simple loop over a provided/implicit collection of item references.

function_map.name: U8P_AIRSPEL::0080
function_map.role: Presence-test helper that scans items and returns boolean indicating if an Air-focus exists (frame==3 + type mask).

function_map.name: U8P_AIRSPEL::00CF
function_map.role: Primary 'use/read focus' handler: mana check/cost, spawn visual/sound effects, award intelligence, build and bark descriptive message, choose breath variant if applicable.

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Engine API returning an item's frame/variant (used to filter Air-focus by frame==3).

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Engine API returning an item's type bitmask (used for type-based filtering).

internal_calls.purpose: Ask whether a qualifying Air-focus item is present to decide on effect variant/behavior.

external_calls.purpose: Read each item's frame/variant to filter for the Air-focus visual/state (frame==3).
Read item type flags/bitmask to identify focus items via mask comparisons.
Obtain caster/NPC map id used to select a location fragment for the focus reading message.
Transform or map the raw map id into a location code/index used to pick message fragments (exact mapping inferred).
Read caster's current mana to determine if the focus can be used.
Write back the reduced mana after charging the focus cost.
Spawn visual/animation effects oriented by caster direction (projectile/cone/etc.).
Spawn helper for playing sound or timed auxiliary effects during the focus use.
Award/increment a small intelligence-related reward when the focus is read.
Get current in-game time to compute time-of-day and day for the message appended to the focus reading.
Convert computed numeric day/time values into strings for message construction.
(Inferred) Return a town/region index or related value used to append a location/town name fragment to the message.
Display the constructed focus-reading string to the player (item or NPC 'speaks' the message).
Obtain caster direction to orient spawned effects correctly.

reads.name: hasBreath
reads.likely_effect: Selects whether a 'breath' variant of the Air effect/spawn sequence should be used instead of the standard sequence.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides logic for detecting and using an "Air" focus item. The small detector (AIRSPEL::0080) scans a collection of items and returns true if any item matches the Air-focus criteria (checks item frame==3 and item type bitmask).
